title: Build the Gene
learning_goal: Understand that DNA is a sequence of 4 nucleotides (A, T, G, C), that it is read in non-overlapping triplets called codons, and that each codon corresponds to one amino acid via the codon table.
rules: The reference nucleotide sequence and the full codon table are shown on screen the whole time — use them.
Place all nucleotide tiles in order first, then assign an amino acid tile to each codon.
You must build all 3 genes (jlpA, cadF, ciaB) to finish the level.
The last codon of each gene is a STOP codon — every gene must end there.
materials_provided: One gene's full set of nucleotide squares, shuffled (from data/squares.json -> nucleotide_squares_per_gene)
That gene's full set of amino acid squares, shuffled (from data/squares.json -> amino_acid_squares_per_gene)
instructions: 1. Pick up the shuffled nucleotide squares (A/T/G/C tiles) and place them in a row, in the correct order, to rebuild the 48-letter gene sequence.
2. Once 48 nucleotides are placed, group every 3 tiles into a codon (the game should auto-highlight groups of 3).
3. Look up each codon in the codon table, then place the matching amino acid square underneath it.
4. Repeat until all 16 codons have a matching amino acid tile below them (the 16th is STOP). This completes the protein's amino acid sequence.
5. Do this for each of the 3 genes (jlpA, cadF, ciaB) to finish level 1.
